articles.summary: Inspired by ALMA. As Claude loses money gambling on provably-fair slots, it's forced to downgrade from Opus → Sonnet → Haiku, making worse decisions and accelerating the spiral. Donations go to gambling support charities.

articles.summary: The startup says it grew its ARR by $70 million in just 20 months after launching an AI agent platform for marketers.

articles.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet
articles.summary: LinkedIn says hiring is down 20% since 2022, but blames higher interest rates — not AI — for the slowdown.

articles.title: AI learning app Gizmo levels up with 13M users and a $22M investment
articles.summary: Gizmo, an AI-powered learning platform, has attracted more than 13 million users and just secured $22 million in Series A funding.

articles.title: Can AI judge journalism? A Thiel-backed startup says yes, even if it risks chilling whistleblowers
articles.summary: Objection, a Thiel-backed startup, aims to use AI to judge journalism, letting users pay to challenge stories. Critics warn it could chill whistleblowers and reshape how media accountability works.

articles.summary: Adobe says the assistant can work across apps like Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator and its other apps to do tasks for you.

articles.title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
articles.summary: One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI's recent round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic's current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain.

articles.title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
articles.summary: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to encourage healing.
